paper_title: Opening Opportunities for High-Resolution Isotope Analysis - Quantification of δ15NNO3 and δ18ONO3 in Diffusive Equilibrium in Thin-Film Passive Samplers.

methods.description: Procedure for equilibrating Diffusive Equilibrium in Thin-Film (DET) gels in solutions with controlled nitrate concentrations for specific time periods to study isotope fractionation during diffusion. This method was applied in laboratory experiments investigating the influence of nitrate concentration and equilibration time on fractionation, including a matrix effects test.

steps.description: Prepare initial solutions with specific nitrate concentrations. This included concentrations up to 7.0 g NO3− L−1 for an initial proof of concept study, and lower concentrations between 20.1 and 100.5 mg NO3− L−1 for a more environmentally relevant experiment. A river sample spiked with nitrate (7.0 ± 0.0 g NO3− L−1) was also used to test for matrix effects; the river sample was collected from the River Tern, U.K. and filtered (0.2 μm).
steps.searchable_summary: prepare nitrate solutions at specified concentrations for DET gel equilibration, including spiking and filtering for matrix tests
outputs: prepared nitrate solutions at various concentrations

steps.description: Equilibrate DET gels in the prepared solutions for different time periods: 24, 48, and 168 h.
steps.searchable_summary: equilibrate DET gels in prepared nitrate solutions for specified durations

steps.description: All experiments were performed in triplicate.
At the end of the equilibration period DET gels were removed from solution.
steps.searchable_summary: remove DET gels from solution after equilibration

steps.description: The concentration of the stock solution was compared to that of the solution in the beaker after the gel was removed. A concentration 97.7% that of the stock solution was expected due to the gel volume being 2.3% of the solution volume. The solution concentrations were found to be 95.0 ± 3.0% that of the stock (n = 12).
steps.searchable_summary: compare post-equilibration solution concentration to stock concentration for validation

methods.description: Procedure for back-equilibrating nitrate from DET gels after they have been removed from the initial solution. (Note: The full procedure is not described in the provided text).
